reasoning: Both models found the critical PKGBUILD safe, and all source code files reviewed show standard behavior for a Rust tool manager. The qwen model's inconclusive verdict was due to a parsing error on a non-executable config file, not a security concern.
disagreement_analysis: Disagreement stemmed from qwen's inability to parse its own audit response for .cargo/config.toml (a non-executable Rust config file), not from any security finding.
coverage_issues: Both models skipped reviewing proto-0.58.0.tar.gz source tarball (though extracted contents were analyzed)
No model reviewed .install hooks if present (none found in reports)
learnings: Improve file prioritization: Models wasted cycles on non-critical files (e2e scripts, package.json, nvchecker.toml) instead of focusing exclusively on build/install hooks and core code
Enhance response parsing: Prevent inconclusive verdicts from model output formatting errors
Handle binary files better: Consider partial extraction/scanning of tarballs instead of full skip
Standardize risk assessment: Clarify that non-executed configs (like .cargo/config.toml) shouldn't trigger inconclusive verdicts
